message:
ui+readme: slide-3 reframe (no editorial 'cost:' tags) + App Store hero img

User flagged the previous rewrite ('cost: a new service / cost: $ per
query / cost: no shared view') was still editorial — the 'cost:' label
just renamed the same put-down. The slide kept reading like 'here are
three things and here's why each is bad'.

Reframe — drop the comparison entirely:
  - tag: 'the usual options' → 'where memory lives today'
  - head: 'Three shapes today. Each has a trade-off.' →
          'Three shapes. Each works for what it was built for.'
  - body: replaces the 'but...' framing with neutral observation:
          'Most teams already use one of these to give agents memory.
           They're all valid; they just answer different questions.
           The next slide is what we noticed was missing in between
           them.'
  - per-card text describes WHAT each thing IS (Vector DB →
    'purpose-built for vector search'; Warehouse → 'where the data
    already lives'; Library → 'embedded in one process'). No costs,
    no judgments.
  - footer: 'all three are useful · all three solve different problems'
    sets up slide 4 ('the missing middle') which is where the
    positioning argument actually belongs.

Plus: README gets a second screenshot (assets/console-appstore.png,
143 KB) above the Substrate adapters section showing the App Store
route with rvDNA + ruQu both SHIPPING, install commands visible, and
the live-MCP pill in the top-right.

files.patch: @@ -784,6 +784,10 @@ cargo test --release -- --ignored gcs_live ### Substrate adapters — IPFS, genomic, quantum + + +> The Console's **App Store** route — every shipped substrate listed with status tag, install commands (Rust crate · MCP companion · npm), and per-card links to its ADR, deep gist, and research dir. Operators can browse what's available at a glance. + Beyond GCS, ruLake plugs into anything that fits the [`BackendAdapter`](src/backend.rs) trait. The standalone-repo strategy ([ADR-001](docs/adrs/ADR-001-standalone-repo-strategy.md)) means every adapter is its own Cargo crate — pin it independently, ship it independently, and reuse it under any other ruLake deployment. | Crate | ADR | Status | What it adds |

@@ -62,35 +62,32 @@ function IntroSlideshow() { ), }, { - tag: 'the usual options', - head: 'Three shapes today. Each has a trade-off.', - body: <>A vector database is purpose-built but means standing up another service and replicating your data into it. A warehouse already holds your data but charges per query. A local library is fast but lives inside one process — there's no shared layer agents on different machines can both verify against., + tag: 'where memory lives today', + head: 'Three shapes. Each works for what it was built for.', + body: <>Most teams already use one of these to give agents memory. They're all valid; they just answer different questions. The next slide is what we noticed was missing in between them., art: ( ), },
